methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceX is a late-stage private company in Space & Aerospace pursuing an IPO, with a stated valuation of $800B and cumulative fundraising of $86B. The comp universe spans 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard valuation triangulation methods face material constraints. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 24 same-sector peers exist, zero same-stage, zero adjacent-stage, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches were identified—the minimum threshold for meaningful comparison is three datapoints. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple cannot proceed because SpaceX's revenue is not on file; the public Space & Aerospace median P/S is 36.9x across the 6-ticker comp set, but application requires disclosed target revenue, which private companies rarely provide. Without either a sufficient peer median or usable revenue figures, traditional quantitative anchoring cannot be executed.

The $800B valuation therefore remains unvalidated against conventional methods in this analysis. Key uncertainties include the lack of comparable same-stage transactions, unavailable financial disclosure from the target, and the extreme valuation tier—well above all public sector comps—which limits meaningful cross-reference. Resolution would require either disclosed SpaceX revenue, identification of additional private IPO-candidate comps at comparable scale, or transaction multiples from recent fundraising rounds.
